verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is canonically a Golgi-resident type II membrane glycosyltransferase, but it has a well-documented dual-localization minority pool at the plasma membrane where it functions as a cell-surface lectin/adhesion molecule. Surface B4GALT1 has been described on sperm (binding ZP3 during fertilization), on neurites mediating cell-cell adhesion, and on various somatic cells where the catalytic ectodomain faces extracellularly via its type II topology. The protein retains its own TM anchor in the PM pool, satisfying the recruitment test. This places it in dual_localization rather than endomembrane_resident-only.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Magnitude/accessibility of the surface pool vs dominant Golgi residence; whether surface display is broad enough to support binder programs beyond germline contexts.
